seoTitle: How to Make a Cover Page on Google Docs Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to design a professional cover page in Google Docs with independent first-page formatting, custom imagery, and clean section breaks.
intro: A polished cover page sets the tone for a report, manuscript, or portfolio, but getting one right in Google Docs means isolating its formatting from the rest of the document. This guide shows you how to use different first-page settings, add title and imagery elements, and insert a section break so your cover stays visually separate from the body text.

steps.heading: Isolate the first page
steps.body: Double-click the header area and check 'Different first page' so your cover page formatting won't carry into the rest of the document.

steps.heading: Design your title elements
steps.body: Use Insert > Drawing to build custom title banners or logos, then type your document title and adjust typography for visual hierarchy.

steps.heading: Add author and date details
steps.body: Center your text and add supporting details like author name, company, and creation date below the title.

steps.heading: Balance the layout with spacing
steps.body: Use line and paragraph spacing tools to push your details cleanly into the lower portion of the page.

steps.heading: Insert a section break
steps.body: Place your cursor at the end of the cover content and go to Insert > Break > Section break (next page) so page numbering starts correctly on page two.

faq.question: Will my page numbers start counting on the cover page?
faq.answer: Not if you use 'Different first page' and a next-page section break together, which lets you exclude the cover from numbering.

faq.question: Can I add a background image to my cover page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can insert an image and adjust its layering and text wrapping to sit behind your title text.

faq.question: Do I need Google Slides to design the cover instead?
faq.answer: No, the built-in Drawing tool inside Docs is enough for most title banners, logos, and dividers.

faq.question: Can I reuse this cover page as a template?
faq.answer: Yes, once formatted you can save the document as a template or duplicate it for future reports.
